summary: Phase 27 retest after 2 HIGH fixes. Backend pytest: 24/25 PASS (1 skipped — SP assigned_partners population path unavailable via public API). Customer email-match RBAC fix on GET /api/leads/{id} is VERIFIED working (3 new tests pass). HOWEVER, two CRITICAL issues block both retest items end-to-end: (a) GET /api/leads/{id} does NOT include `deal_room_enabled`/`deal_room_opened_at` in the response — LeadResponse model is missing these fields. Toggle returns 200 with enabled=true and DB persists, but the lead GET keeps returning deal_room_enabled=None, so `!!lead?.deal_room_enabled` stays false in DealRoomTab.jsx → admin UI never flips even though the fix at lines 52-63 awaits onLeadRefresh + calls loadDealRoom. (b) Customer email-match RBAC widening leaks internal fields: GET /api/leads/{id} now returns the FULL lead (deal_value=500000, description='Internal info…', etc.) to the customer. The /deal-room endpoint correctly hides these, but the regular lead GET exposes them. Verified via curl on lead 59ad1b49-0e02-4689-8cd9-27f1f951b239 as john@testco.com.

critical.issue: Response is MISSING `deal_room_enabled` and `deal_room_opened_at` fields. Verified: toggle returns 200 {enabled:true}, DB persists (confirmed via /deal-room which sees lead.deal_room_enabled=True), but lead GET returns deal_room_enabled=None. Root cause: LeadResponse Pydantic model (or enrich_lead) does not include these fields. This causes the entire admin UI-flip flow to fail because the parent re-fetch returns enabled=None → false.

Customer email-match RBAC widening (server.py:3088-3093) leaks internal fields. Customer john@testco.com gets full lead payload incl. deal_value=500000.0, description='Internal info…'. The deal-room endpoint hides these correctly, but the main lead GET does not. Either strip internal fields when access is via email-match (not created_by), or have customer UI use only /deal-room data.

ui_bugs.issue: After admin clicks 'Open Deal Room', toast 'Deal Room opened — customer can now collaborate.' appears (toggle succeeds), and toggleDealRoom awaits onLeadRefresh + calls loadDealRoom — BUT disabled-card still rendered, deal-room-tab never appears. Root cause is the backend issue above (deal_room_enabled missing from LeadResponse), NOT a frontend bug per se. As a defensive frontend fix, derive enabled from `(!!lead?.deal_room_enabled) || !!data` OR keep a local `optimisticEnabled` state after a successful toggle.

Customer now reaches /leads/{id} (RBAC fix), but the page renders the FULL admin layout including 'Internal info' header, Deal Value ₹5,00,000, Category, AI Insights, Commission Breakdown, Predictive Forecast. Customer should see a stripped-down Deal-Room-only view. Right now backend leaks internal fields and frontend renders them all.

integration_issues.flow: Admin Open Deal Room → UI flip
integration_issues.issue: End-to-end broken because backend GET /leads/{id} omits deal_room_enabled. Fix backend first.

Customer GET /leads/{id} now returns 200 (good) but exposes deal_value/description (bad). Plus, deal_room_enabled=None in the response means the customer also sees the disabled-card 'Deal Room not yet opened by the owner' lock — even though backend DB has enabled=true. Customer cannot reach the message input / approval buttons.

action_items: CRITICAL (backend): Add `deal_room_enabled: Optional[bool] = False` and `deal_room_opened_at: Optional[datetime] = None` (and any other Phase-27 fields) to LeadResponse model, OR ensure enrich_lead() copies these from the DB doc. Without this, NO admin can ever see the live Deal Room view via UI — the disabled card is permanent.

CRITICAL (backend): Strip internal lead fields (description, deal_value, internal_notes, commission_breakdown source, etc.) from GET /api/leads/{id} response when the access is granted via customer-email-match (i.e., lead.created_by != user.id but lead.customer_email == user.email). Mirror the redaction logic from the /deal-room endpoint. OR add a dedicated /api/leads/{id}/customer-view endpoint.

MEDIUM (frontend defensive): In DealRoomTab.jsx, change `const enabled = !!lead?.deal_room_enabled` to also be true when a successful toggle just happened, e.g., maintain `localEnabled` state set by toggleDealRoom on success — so the UI is not at the mercy of the parent re-fetch + LeadResponse shape.

MEDIUM (frontend): For customer role on /leads/:id, render a Deal-Room-only layout (no Internal info, Commission Breakdown, AI Insights, Predictive Forecast sidebar) — gate by user.role === 'customer'.
critical_code_review_comments: LeadResponse model (Pydantic) appears to NOT declare deal_room_enabled / deal_room_opened_at — verify with `grep -n deal_room /app/backend/server.py | head -40`. Any field not declared on response_model is silently dropped by FastAPI.

RBAC widening at server.py:3088-3093 grants full lead access to email-match customers but does NOT redact internal fields — this is a privacy/data-leak regression. The deal-room endpoint correctly redacts via is_internal_viewer; the same logic should apply here.

DealRoomTab.toggleDealRoom fix relies on parent prop propagation (`enabled = !!lead?.deal_room_enabled`). Since the backend response omits the field, the fix is functionally dead. A local optimistic state is the safer pattern.
Selling-partner assigned_partners RBAC (server.py:3082-3085) is code-correct; couldn't fully E2E-verify because LeadCreate/LeadUpdate schemas do not appear to accept assigned_partners in payload.

success_rate.backend: 100% on declared scope (24/25 PASS, 1 skip). But 2 CRITICAL real-world bugs found via integration testing (missing field + internal-field leak)
success_rate.frontend: 0% on retest items — admin UI flip still broken (root cause is backend), customer reaches lead but sees internal fields + still sees disabled-card lock

seed_data_creation: Reused seed lead 59ad1b49-0e02-4689-8cd9-27f1f951b239. Re-toggled deal_room to enabled=true via API (DB confirmed via /deal-room read). Multiple TEST_Phase27 module-scoped leads created by pytest.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two CRITICAL backend issues block both Phase-27 user flows: (1) LeadResponse missing deal_room_enabled — verify by `curl GET /api/leads/{id}` and check the JSON keys; toggle returns ok=true but the field never surfaces in lead GET. (2) Customer email-match RBAC leaks internal fields (deal_value, description) on /leads/{id} — verify by logging in as john@testco.com and curling /api/leads/59ad1b49-0e02-4689-8cd9-27f1f951b239. Pytest file at /app/backend/tests/test_phase27_deal_room.py has 25 tests; cookie-disabled session helper is mandatory due to Cloudflare __cf_bm bleed.
rca of the issue:
Iteration 14 reported 2 HIGH issues. E1's fix narrative:
  (a) DealRoomTab.toggleDealRoom now awaits onLeadRefresh AND calls loadDealRoom — verified in code at lines 52-63.
  (b) Lead GET RBAC widened for customer email-match — verified in code at server.py:3088-3093.
Both code changes are in place. BUT integration testing reveals two NEW/exposed bugs:
  1. LeadResponse Pydantic model omits deal_room_enabled — confirmed by `requests.get(/api/leads/{id})` returning {deal_room_enabled: None} immediately after a successful POST /toggle that returns {enabled: true}. So onLeadRefresh fetches the lead but `lead.deal_room_enabled` is undefined, keeping `enabled = false` → disabled card permanent.
  2. The RBAC widen is too permissive — customer email-match grants full lead read with all internal fields. The /deal-room endpoint properly redacts (description=None, deal_value=None for is_internal_viewer=false), but the main lead GET does not.
Reproduction:
  $ curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer <admin>' -X POST /api/leads/<id>/deal-room/toggle -d '{"enabled":true}'  → 200 {enabled:true}
  $ curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer <admin>' /api/leads/<id>  → deal_room_enabled is NOT in response keys
  $ curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer <customer>' /api/leads/<id>  → 200 with deal_value=500000, description='Internal info', etc.
Mitigation:
  - Add deal_room_enabled + deal_room_opened_at to LeadResponse model (and any companion fields like deal_room_closed_at).
  - In get_lead, when access is granted via customer-email-match (not created_by), redact internal fields before returning (mirror _can_access_deal_room redaction).
